title: lophopyxidaceae
intro: The Lophopyxidaceae rank refers to a family of plants, specifically a group of fungi in the division Ascomycota. This family is characterized by its small to medium-sized species, often with distinctive fruiting bodies and a diverse range of habitats. Lophopyxidaceae includes species that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions, and are often associated with decaying organic matter. The family's name is derived from the Greek words "lophos," meaning ridge, and "pyxis," meaning box, likely referring to the distinctive shape of its fruiting bodies.
